title: Depreciation Methods: The depreciation expense for a piece of machinery was calculated...
summary: Recalculate depreciation expense after a revision in estimated useful life.
prompt: The depreciation expense for a piece of machinery was calculated to be $10,000 per year over a 10-year useful life. If the company decides to change its useful life to 15 years after 5 years, what will be the new annual depreciation expense?
solution: Step 1: Calculate total depreciation taken = $10,000 * 5 = $50,000. Step 2: Remaining book value = Purchase Price - Accumulated Depreciation. If the initial purchase was $100,000, new remaining value = $100,000 - $50,000 = $50,000. Step 3: New Annual Depreciation = Remaining Value / New Useful Life = $50,000 / 10 = $5,000.
